action.hypothesis: After 21 no-improve rounds and failures from plain top-k, routed, gated, bucketed, discrete, and low-rank branches at `K=24`, the next severe-stagnation move should test a fundamentally different input-space transformation rather than another support-allocation variant. `whitened_topk` at `K=24, EF=12` may improve mid-K reconstruction by making the activation space easier to sparsify before hard selection, potentially recovering a better FVU/K tradeoff than the recent untransformed baselines.
action.summary: Run a pure architecture comparison at `K=24, EF=12` with `whitened_topk` under the pinned recipe `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`. This follows severe-stagnation policy by trying another genuinely different built-in family instead of any scalar sweep or code repair, while keeping the experiment anchored on the frontier-relevant mid-K slot.
action.self_review: I avoided more support-selection and low-rank variants because those mechanisms have already failed repeatedly in this stagnation phase. `whitened_topk` is a cleaner architecture jump: it changes the input representation rather than the sparsity rule, while keeping the round architecture-only with all recipe fields pinned.
notes_to_memory: Round 57 continues severe-stagnation recovery with a pure `whitened_topk` architecture test after plain `topk` also failed badly at K=24.
Keep the run pinned at `K=24, EF=12, signum, LR=3.2e-3, AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` so architecture is the only primary variable.
`whitened_topk` tests whether input-space normalization/preconditioning helps the mid-K regime more than the recent untransformed support-selection, low-rank, and discrete families.
next_hypotheses: If `whitened_topk` is competitive but slightly worse at K=24, test it at K=16 to see whether preconditioning helps more under tighter sparsity.
If `whitened_topk` clearly loses at K=24, retire this preconditioned branch and move to another genuinely different built-in family rather than any scalar sweep.
If `whitened_topk` wins at K=24, follow with K=16 or K=32 to map whether the gain generalizes across the mid-K band.

result.description: Run a pure architecture comparison at `K=24, EF=12` with `whitened_topk` under the pinned recipe `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`. This follows severe-stagnation policy by trying another genuinely different built-in family instead of any scalar sweep or code repair, while keeping the experiment anchored on the frontier-relevant mid-K slot.
